reviews.review_description: All this game really needs is, of course for the multiplayer to be fully functional a bit of graphics polishing and expand it out. Also, work on the hand tracking because with 30 seconds per turn you cant afford to miss grab a card. Because it's a really good great game. It is excruciatingly, it's difficult to find a good card battle game and let alone in the vr world.

I would give it 5 stars if I could actually use the multiplayer,you cant join a game at all,you have to host one and I've gotten one game in it,I think against one of the developers and it was awesome but I had to sit there for a good probably hour till it finally happened and realistically that's crazy and not something a normal person is going to do.

Multiplayer works, you just need a friend who plays at the same time.

It would be great if the multiplayer also had voice chat.

+ excellent graphics
+ Good tutorial
- i had a hard time to grab a card from the hand
- i could not see what the card do before i could grab it

Sorry, but hearthstone requires quick thinking and hand movements. In my case, everything was akward
looks so good...there is no way of adjusting chair height that i could find making it impossible for me to play...if there is a way please advise...
This game so far is really fun and looking forward to watching it grow!!
This game is the first in what will become an interconnected universe of VR games in the world of Sun Shard. And I, for one, am excited

reviews.review_title: Potentially great, but being held back.
reviews.review_description: This game is great! The card art is awesome and the ambience is too cool, also being able to do private rooms is great. My issues with this game are few but they do somewhat hold this game back. First off it’s somewhat difficult to get a card out of your hand, takes a lot of messing around, and that is a huge issue when your turn is only 30 seconds. Which os another issue, 30 second turns are too short when learning a card game and trying to read abilities and also deal with a hand that’s difficult to pull cards out of. Once your opponent plays cards and abilities you cannot read that creature or it’s abilities. Definitely a cool game but needs some fine tuning.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback! We've just pushed an update addressing a couple of your concerns, and we're still working hard on some of the others. Feel free to update your game and try it out!

reviews.review_description: Would be so much better if multiplayer actially worked?

Please can you make it so that I can adjust my view. For some reason the desk is neck height for me so I can’t play sitting down, or for some reason I spawn behind the chair so I can’t even see the table.. pressing the oculus button puts me in the middle of the table neck high 😬

I love this game though, it’s very addictive, i like how it was easy to learn, with free distractions and I enjoy the visuals. It’s a shame I can’t play with other online users though as the ‘join match’ function doesn’t work. 

Please fix the height of the table though- it’s such a simple thing, but playing the game depends on it.

I played the AI several times and found it enjoyable. It took me a bit of time to figure out how to grab the cards, I found don the third AI game the white hand showing me which card I was grabbing. The white hand is helpful but hard to see at first.  I felt the timer on the AI was not necessary because it is a practice area. If there was not a time limit in the practice area with the AI I would be able to learn the cards better and the abilities per card so when I play an actual game the time limit would not be a big deal because I would have learned the cards in the AI practice. It would be cool to see more actual card fighting instead of just a card bump. The cards that have shields on them that require the player to target them first should have a different color rim on them so they stick out better in my hand and on the table. Provoke is a great feature but needs to be more prominently displayed in the hand and on the table. I have not played a live game yet BUT my Grandson is coming over tomorrow and we will try to play each other. This game is not bad at all and I look forward to future upgrades. Keep up the good work, develop more complex cards, hold tournaments, add more fighting in card sequence, maybe add some dice to options to buff cards. I have not played enough to see the other options that become available when one wins more games. i will be back once I play more to update my review.

A fun game in VR that I can actually SIT down, RELAX, and play.  This is a (very) simplified version of Magic: TG that is played in an ultra-cool dungeon-like environment.  

Music is sparse however, and creature/lighting/special sound/animation/ effects are practically non-existent.  There is also currently only ONE totally personality-devoid AI NPC to play against, in AI mode.  ...He/she DOES LOOK cool, though.  So there IS that.  

--The game is quite SIMPLE and LIMITED right now, is the main point.

However, if you are curious and are reading this review, do NOT let those no-doubt TEMPORARY limitations, which are ONLY due to a combination of the game's independent development status and current stage of developmental PROGRESS, stop you from getting it.  It is VERY fun and, due to highly effective deck/hand randomization, it can already be played over and over (especially if you're quarantined, like I am right now)!

-PLUS, it's FREE, and it ALSO takes up VERY little storage space in its present state.  Far less than a single gigabyte, I think-?  Maybe even less than half of one; I can't recall.  Either way, it's tiny, and your limited-space Quest library will thank you.

If you're like me, and you love LIBERATING SOULS trapped in Purgatory with a holy bow in In Death: Unchained, and SHOVING SPEARS through enemy SKULLS in Battle Talent, but after a while of sweating to these more physically-demanding games, you STILL want to be a total nerd and keep nerding-it-up, but might need a little BREAK, then THIS game is definitely what you NEED!

**A few (obvious) suggestions for the devs (stuff I'm SURE you're ALREADY working on):

1) Show/animate the creatures and spells we cast via the cards, actually making it so they are REALLY being "conjured" onto the table!  Animate creature attacks and abilities, too, and card spells, with appropriate lighting-effects and such!

2) Add more play environments and NPC looks/personality types/more avatars for human players and online opponents.

3) Add NPC tournaments (complete with background-onlookers!) where we can WIN cool special cards and stuff every once in a while.  Or maybe just add gold, that we could BET, and therefore win - OR lose! We could use the gold to PURCHASE cool, rare cards or deck-modifiers.

4) Add more MUSIC.  That ONE piece of music is actually cool, it just gets a little repetitive.

5)  Add cool effects/animation when we WIN, or, for that matter, LOSE, a game.

These are only my humble suggestions (BRILLIANT as they ARE 😜), once again, and everything ELSE about this app, from the overall design, the atmosphere, the graphics, etc, INCLUDING having a MAGIC, SEVERED-HAND hold your cards, is FANTASTIC.  What you already DO have here has been really, really KNOCKED out of the PARK, truly.

You guys are developing something TRULY SPECIAL, here.  PLEASE keep at it!  -and, THANK you!! 

I can't WAIT to see what you DO with this GREAT game in the future!!

What a fun experience. Obviously there are some issues currently, which I'm sure you are aware of and will be addressing soon. I have to say though, what a great game. My gf and I just played through 2 games together and had a blast. We didn't play the how to play, but for the most part we have it figured out. Maybe a wrist menu compendium would be cool, with tactics and gameplay tips, stats, general how to play etc that can be accessed at any point during the game at the user's discretion would be a cool implementation. I like the music, but already after 2 games, we need more music. I'm sure attack and damage sounds are in the future, but ambient music needs to be remedied also. Speaking of ambiance, you guys absolutely killed it. The dungeon-y castle theme is absolutely epic and we loved it. I am fully aware of the pre-release, beta, early access, not yet a full game nature of this release and I can say with confidence that this game is going to be great. Please add an option for private games, we are currently able to find each other reliably, but once this game takes off it will be a nightmare trying to play together. Way to go devs, you really have some potential here.
it wont let me join a online match

reviews.review_title: Cool idea but can only play ai
reviews.review_description: Iv tried to find matches several weeks in a row and it seems no developer progress,  so im over it unfortunately. Seems abandoned, there's no point in releasing a multiplayer game before multi is enabled,  all you do is lose players

I still want a yugioh vr game. But this fills that a little. I battled the AI about a million times. It really got me excited and into it. I hope or wish there's a campaign or story mode and build decks and all. I really love this game! I fully support and recommend it to anyone!
